prompt.content:
---
module: incremental
deps: [schemas_attribution, schemas_line_range]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: diffing two prompt bodies tracks the changed line and emits a unified diff
    given: { old: "Greet a person by name.\\n", new: "Greet a person by name, loudly.\\n" }
    expect: { unified_contains: ["-Greet a person by name.", "+Greet a person by name, loudly."], changed_old_lines: [1], changed_new_lines: [1] }
  - name: the unlock list is sorted and deduplicated
    given: { owned: [".napl/src/typescript/greet.ts"], intersecting: [], target_rel: ".napl/src/typescript" }
    expect: { list: [".napl/src/typescript/greet.ts"] }
---
# Incremental regeneration: body-line diffing and the unlock list

This module computes what an *incremental* (diff-scoped) regeneration must touch:
it diffs two versions of a prompt body with per-line change tracking, selects the
owned attribution regions that intersect the changed lines, and builds the sorted
unlock list of files to reopen. It is pure: no I/O.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `incremental/`: `incremental/Cargo.toml` (package
name `incremental`) and `incremental/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside
`incremental/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root before
finishing.

## Builds on two modules of this workspace

Both are sibling member crates generated into this same workspace. Add a path
dependency on each in your `Cargo.toml` (workspace siblings, not external
crates.io dependencies), use their public API, and do **not** reimplement their
logic or depend on any hand-written crate.

- **`schemas_line_range`** (`../schemas_line_range`): exposes `LineRange`, an
  inclusive 1-based range with public `start` and `end` fields (`u32`) and a
  `new(start, end)` constructor.
- **`schemas_attribution`** (`../schemas_attribution`): exposes
  `AttributionEntry` (`Clone`), a mapping from prompt lines to code lines with
  public fields `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `file: String`, `lines: LineRange`,
  and `note: String`. This module reads `prompt_lines` and `file`, and returns
  cloned `AttributionEntry` values.

## The body-line diff

Expose a public struct `BodyLineDiff` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`) with
public fields `unified: String`, `changed_old_lines: Vec<usize>`, and
`changed_new_lines: Vec<usize>`.

`diff_body_lines(old_body: &str, new_body: &str) -> BodyLineDiff` computes a
line-level diff between the two bodies:

- Split each body into lines on `\n`, stripping a trailing `\r` from each line
  (so `"a\nb\n"` splits into `["a", "b", ""]`).
- Compute a longest-common-subsequence alignment of the two line sequences,
  producing an ordered list of operations, each an **equal**, a **deletion** (a
  line present only in the old body), or an **insertion** (a line present only in
  the new body). Deletions carry a 1-based old-body line number; insertions carry
  a 1-based new-body line number; equals carry both. When the choice between a
  deletion and an insertion is free (neither extends the common subsequence
  further), prefer the deletion.
- `changed_old_lines` is the old-body line numbers of the deletions, in order.
- `changed_new_lines` is the new-body line numbers of the insertions, in order.
- `unified` is a unified diff of the two bodies with **3 lines of context**: group
  the operations into hunks, where a hunk covers every operation within 3 of a
  change; a purely-equal run wider than the context splits hunks. Each hunk begins
  with a header `@@ -oldStart,oldCount +newStart,newCount @@`, where `oldStart`
  and `oldCount` count the lines in the hunk present in the old body (equals and
  deletions) and `newStart`/`newCount` those present in the new body (equals and
  insertions); `oldStart`/`newStart` are the first such line number in the hunk,
  or `0` if none. Under each header, one line per operation: a leading space then
  the text for an equal, a leading `-` then the old text for a deletion, a leading
  `+` then the new text for an insertion. Join all hunk lines with `\n` (no
  trailing newline). So changing the sole body line from
  `Greet a person by name.` to `Greet a person by name, loudly.` yields a unified
  diff containing the lines `-Greet a person by name.` and
  `+Greet a person by name, loudly.`.

## Selecting the intersecting regions

`select_intersecting_entries(entries: &[AttributionEntry], changed_old_lines: &[usize]) -> Vec<AttributionEntry>`:
keep (cloned) every entry whose prompt range intersects the changed old lines —
that is, some line in the inclusive range `prompt_lines.start ..= prompt_lines.end`
(compared as `usize`) appears in `changed_old_lines`. Preserve input order.

## The unlock list

`incremental_unlock_list(owned_files: &[String], intersecting_entries: &[AttributionEntry], target_rel_to_root: &str) -> Vec<String>`:
start from the owned files, then for each intersecting entry add the string
`"{target_rel_to_root}/{file}"` (the entry's `file` joined under the target-
relative directory with a `/`). Sort the resulting list and remove duplicates,
returning the sorted, deduplicated vector.

name = "incremental"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
schemas_line_range = { path = "../schemas_line_range" }
schemas_attribution = { path = "../schemas_attribution" }

attribution.note: BodyLineDiff struct with three public fields
group ops into hunks within context
prefix lines per op and join
select entries whose prompt range intersects changed lines
seed owned files, add target-joined entry files

mapl.message: No changes — prompt unchanged since prior compile, existing code already satisfies it.
mapl.reasoning: Agent reported "Empty prompt diff, code already matches prompt. All 11 tests pass." This is an incremental regeneration where the prompt body did not differ from the previously compiled version, so the existing `incremental/` crate (BodyLineDiff, diff_body_lines, select_intersecting_entries, incremental_unlock_list) already implements every requirement and its test suite passes. No edits were warranted.

mapl.message: Unified-diff hunk-grouping rules were not exercised by new work but carry latent ambiguity.
mapl.reasoning: The spec for grouping operations into hunks ("a hunk covers every operation within 3 of a change; a purely-equal run wider than the context splits hunks") is prose that admits more than one boundary interpretation at hunk edges and around adjacent changes. Since no changes were made, the existing implementation's interpretation stands unverified against any new intent — worth confirming against the passing tests if hunk boundaries matter.

maplContent:
module: incremental
target: rust
entries:
  - promptLines:
      - 1
      - 82
    kind: no-op
    message: No changes — prompt unchanged since prior compile, existing code already satisfies it.
    reasoning: Agent reported "Empty prompt diff, code already matches prompt. All 11 tests pass." This is an incremental regeneration where the prompt body did not differ from the previously compiled version, so the existing `incremental/` crate (BodyLineDiff, diff_body_lines, select_intersecting_entries, incremental_unlock_list) already implements every requirement and its test suite passes. No edits were warranted.
  - promptLines:
      - 53
      - 62
    kind: note
    message: Unified-diff hunk-grouping rules were not exercised by new work but carry latent ambiguity.
    reasoning: The spec for grouping operations into hunks ("a hunk covers every operation within 3 of a change; a purely-equal run wider than the context splits hunks") is prose that admits more than one boundary interpretation at hunk edges and around adjacent changes. Since no changes were made, the existing implementation's interpretation stands unverified against any new intent — worth confirming against the passing tests if hunk boundaries matter.

---
module: incremental
deps: [schemas_attribution, schemas_line_range]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: diffing two prompt bodies tracks the changed line and emits a unified diff
    given: { old: "Greet a person by name.\\n", new: "Greet a person by name, loudly.\\n" }
    expect: { unified_contains: ["-Greet a person by name.", "+Greet a person by name, loudly."], changed_old_lines: [1], changed_new_lines: [1] }
  - name: the unlock list is sorted and deduplicated
    given: { owned: [".napl/src/typescript/greet.ts"], intersecting: [], target_rel: ".napl/src/typescript" }
    expect: { list: [".napl/src/typescript/greet.ts"] }
---
# Incremental regeneration: body-line diffing and the unlock list

This module computes what an *incremental* (diff-scoped) regeneration must touch:
it diffs two versions of a prompt body with per-line change tracking, selects the
owned attribution regions that intersect the changed lines, and builds the sorted

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `incremental/`: `incremental/Cargo.toml` (package
name `incremental`) and `incremental/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside
`incremental/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root before
finishing.

## Builds on two modules of this workspace

Both are sibling member crates generated into this same workspace. Add a path
dependency on each in your `Cargo.toml` (workspace siblings, not external

logic or depend on any hand-written crate.

- **`schemas_line_range`** (`../schemas_line_range`): exposes `LineRange`, an

- **`schemas_attribution`** (`../schemas_attribution`): exposes

  public fields `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `file: String`, `lines: LineRange`,

  cloned `AttributionEntry` values.

## The body-line diff

Expose a public struct `BodyLineDiff` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`) with

`diff_body_lines(old_body: &str, new_body: &str) -> BodyLineDiff` computes a
line-level diff between the two bodies:

- Split each body into lines on `\n`, stripping a trailing `\r` from each line
  (so `"a\nb\n"` splits into `["a", "b", ""]`).
- Compute a longest-common-subsequence alignment of the two line sequences,

- `unified` is a unified diff of the two bodies with **3 lines of context**: group

  `Greet a person by name.` to `Greet a person by name, loudly.` yields a unified
  diff containing the lines `-Greet a person by name.` and
  `+Greet a person by name, loudly.`.

## Selecting the intersecting regions

`select_intersecting_entries(entries: &[AttributionEntry], changed_old_lines: &[usize]) -> Vec<AttributionEntry>`:
keep (cloned) every entry whose prompt range intersects the changed old lines —
that is, some line in the inclusive range `prompt_lines.start ..= prompt_lines.end`
(compared as `usize`) appears in `changed_old_lines`. Preserve input order.

## The unlock list

`incremental_unlock_list(owned_files: &[String], intersecting_entries: &[AttributionEntry], target_rel_to_root: &str) -> Vec<String>`:
start from the owned files, then for each intersecting entry add the string
`"{target_rel_to_root}/{file}"` (the entry's `file` joined under the target-
relative directory with a `/`). Sort the resulting list and remove duplicates,
